figures.caption: Table 2: Accuracy on the test data (%). The Table shows the accuracy on each acquisition device (CP, CNP, NCP) and the average (Avg) value for different federated learning strategies. If pretraining is not used, we start from standard ImageNet initialization without public-data pretraining.
Fig. 2: Samples from ISIC (top) and their generated counterparts (bottom).
Table 4: Ablation study on the number of active clients per round.
Table 3: Performances varying the total number of clients.
Fig. 3: Examples of the nevus class acquired using different imaging devices.

Fig. 1: Architecture of our federated multi-domain classification approach (FedSSG). On the left, the server-side pretraining, where the classifier and the diffusion model are trained on public mixed-sensor data. In the middle, the persensor organization of the clients, and federated training initialization. On the right, a schematic view of local training and federated aggregation.
